description: Fix the Booking.com CSRF token being cached in the auth cache without a CSRF-specific invalidation path: when the token rotates, write operations fail with 403 and the cache is never cleared (only UNAUTHENTICATED errors clear it), causing all subsequent mutations to also fail until the user reloads.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/booking && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Clear auth cache on CSRF-related 403 errors in booking-api.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/booking/src/booking-api.ts, the GraphQL error handler at lines 113-120 only calls clearAuthCache('booking') for UNAUTHENTICATED or 401 errors. If the CSRF token becomes stale (Booking.com returns a 403 with a CSRF_INVALID or similar message), the cache is not cleared and all future mutations also fail with 403. Update the error handler to also clear the auth cache on 403-type errors so the next request re-fetches a fresh CSRF token from the SSR store.
acceptanceCriteria: The GraphQL error handler clears the auth cache on 403-type errors (not just UNAUTHENTICATED/401)
After the cache is cleared, the next mutation call re-reads the CSRF token from the SSR store via getAuth()
UNAUTHENTICATED and 401 error handling is unchanged
